engineGuide.h2: Aston Martin Vanquish Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Aston Martin Vanquish across both generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 5.9L Petrol
entries.history: First-generation Vanquish V12 (2001-2004), 460bhp, established Aston's modern flagship grand tourer credentials.
commonFailures: Exhaust manifold cracks - cast iron units develop hairline fractures causing ticking noise and emissions failures.
Coil pack degradation - all 12 ignition coils prone to heat-related failure after 60k miles.
Wet sump oil surge - under high G-forces, oil starvation can cause bottom-end bearing failure.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AM3 Aston Martin Vanquish engine replacement

entries.title: 5.9L Petrol
entries.history: Uprated first-gen V12 (2004-2007), 520bhp via forged internals and revised camshafts for Vanquish S.
commonFailures: Exhaust manifold cracks - same cast iron vulnerability as AM3, common on high-mileage Gen 1 cars.
Coil pack failure - 12 coils per engine, heat degradation causes misfires and rough running.
Timing chain tensioner wear - early units may develop rattle; preventative replacement recommended.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AM4 Aston Martin Vanquish engine replacement

entries.title: 5.9L Petrol
entries.history: Second-generation Vanquish V12 (2012-2014), 565bhp with revised cylinder heads and higher compression.
commonFailures: Variable cam timing actuator wear - Gen 2 VCT units can fail causing poor idle and power loss.
Timing chain tensioner wear - early Gen 2 units may develop rattle at 60-80k miles.
Secondary air injection valve failure - emissions component prone to sticking, causing MOT failure.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AM11 Aston Martin Vanquish engine replacement

entries.title: 5.9L Petrol
entries.history: Mid-cycle Gen 2 V12 (2014-2016), 573bhp via ECU calibration only, mechanically identical to AM11.
compatibleVariants: Vanquish (Gen 2 Late), Vanquish Volante (Gen 2 Late), Vanquish Carbon Edition
commonFailures: Variable cam timing actuator wear - same VCT vulnerability as AM11, requires specialist diagnosis.
Secondary air injection valve failure - emissions system component prone to sticking.
ECU calibration sensitivity - power output dependent on precise mapping; improper tuning risks damage.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AM28 Aston Martin Vanquish engine replacement

entries.title: 5.9L Petrol
entries.history: Final Vanquish V12 (2016-2018), 595-603bhp highest output via revised intake, exhaust and valvetrain.
compatibleVariants: Vanquish S (Gen 2), Vanquish Zagato, Vanquish Ultimarte
commonFailures: High-performance valvetrain wear - aggressive cam profiles and sodium-filled valves require specialist maintenance.
ECU calibration sensitivity - 603bhp output dependent on precise mapping; improper tuning can cause knock.
Limited parts availability - Zagato/Ultimarte-specific components have longer lead times for rebuilds.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AM29 Aston Martin Vanquish engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Aston Martin Vanquish Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Aston Martin Vanquish's 6.0-litre V12 is a masterpiece of engineering, but like all high-performance engines, it has known failure points. The AM3/AM4 (Gen 1) and AM11/AM28/AM29 (Gen 2) families share vulnerabilities including wet sump oil surge, coil pack degradation, and timing chain wear. Every rebuilt V12 supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Wet Sump Oil Surge - Why Track-Driven Vanquish Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin Vanquish, Vanquish S, Vanquish Volante (2001-2018, AM3/AM4/AM11/AM28/AM29)
problems.rootCause: The Vanquish V12 uses a wet sump lubrication system. Under sustained high-G cornering or braking, oil can surge away from the pickup, causing momentary starvation and bearing damage.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Accusump Installation - Preventative oil surge protection
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit external oil accumulator to maintain pressure during surge events
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for occasional track use; does not eliminate risk under extreme conditions

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Dry Sump Conversion - Full lubrication system upgrade
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wet sump with external tank, scavenge pumps, and revised plumbing
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates oil surge risk; recommended for regular track or competition use

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned V12 - Catastrophic failure repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt V12 with upgraded bearings, pistons, and oil system components
repairOptions.longevity: Restores reliability; includes 12-month warranty; upgraded components address original failure point

problems.recommendation: If you use your Vanquish on track, fit an accusump (£800-£1,200) as preventative insurance. For regular track use, a dry sump conversion is the only reliable solution. If oil starvation has already caused bearing damage, a rebuilt V12 with upgraded internals is the only viable repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt AM3/AM4/AM11 Aston Martin Vanquish engine prices

problems.h4: Coil Pack Failure - Why Vanquish V12s Develop Misfires
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin Vanquish, Vanquish S, Vanquish Volante (2001-2018, all engine codes)
problems.rootCause: The Vanquish V12 uses 12 individual coil packs (one per cylinder). Heat degradation and age cause insulation breakdown, leading to misfires, rough running, and unburnt fuel washing cylinder bores.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Single Coil Replacement - Address isolated misfire
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace one failed coil pack with OEM-specification unit
repairOptions.longevity: Short-term fix; other coils likely to fail soon due to age

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Full Coil Set Replacement - Preventative maintenance
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all 12 coil packs with upgraded units; includes labour and calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended at 60k miles; prevents cascade failures and cylinder damage

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt V12 - Post-failure repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: If misfires caused cylinder scoring or piston damage, full rebuild required
repairOptions.longevity: Only option if unburnt fuel has damaged cylinders; includes upgraded components

problems.recommendation: Replace all 12 coil packs as a set at 60k miles (£800-£1,500) rather than waiting for failures. Ignoring misfires risks cylinder damage that turns a £1,500 repair into a £20,000+ rebuild.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Aston Martin Vanquish coil pack replacement or V12 rebuild

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear - Why Vanquish Engines Develop Rattles
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin Vanquish, Vanquish S, Vanquish Volante (2001-2018, all engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic timing chain tensioners can lose pressure with age, allowing chain slack. This causes a metallic rattle on cold start and, if ignored, can lead to timing jump and catastrophic valve-to-piston contact.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Tensioner Replacement Only - Address early rattle
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace hydraulic tensioners and guides; chain retained if within spec
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early; does not address chain stretch

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Full Timing Kit - Comprehensive preventative service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioners, guides, chain, and associated components
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended at 70k miles; restores factory timing precision

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt V12 - Post-failure repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: If timing jump caused valve damage, full rebuild with upgraded components required
repairOptions.longevity: Only option if interference damage occurred; includes warranty

problems.recommendation: If you hear a cold-start rattle, address it immediately. A £3,500 timing kit service is far cheaper than a £25,000 rebuild caused by ignored warning signs.
problems.cta: Compare Aston Martin Vanquish timing chain service vs rebuilt engine costs

problems.h4: Exhaust Manifold Cracks - Why Gen 1 Vanquish Engines Tick
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin Vanquish, Vanquish S, Vanquish Volante (2001-2007, AM3/AM4)
problems.rootCause: First-generation Vanquish exhaust manifolds are cast iron and prone to thermal fatigue cracking. Cracks cause a ticking noise, exhaust leaks, and MOT emissions failures.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Manifold Weld Repair - Temporary fix for small cracks
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Specialist welding to seal hairline cracks; not suitable for large fractures
repairOptions.longevity: Short-term solution; cracks often reappear due to thermal cycling

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Aftermarket Stainless Replacement - Permanent repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cracked cast manifolds with stainless steel aftermarket units
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended permanent fix; eliminates recurrence risk and improves flow

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt V12 - Not applicable
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Manifold cracks do not typically cause internal engine damage
repairOptions.longevity: Manifold repair is separate from engine rebuild; address independently

problems.recommendation: Replace cracked Gen 1 manifolds with stainless steel units (£1,800-£3,000) rather than repeated weld repairs. This is a permanent fix that also improves exhaust flow and reduces under-bonnet heat.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Aston Martin Vanquish exhaust manifold replacement

problems.h4: Variable Cam Timing Actuator Failure - Gen 2 Vanquish Power Loss
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin Vanquish, Vanquish Volante (2012-2018, AM11/AM28/AM29)
problems.rootCause: Second-generation Vanquish V12s use variable cam timing (VCT) actuators to optimise valve timing. Internal wear or oil contamination can cause actuators to stick, triggering fault codes, poor idle, and power loss.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Actuator Cleaning/Reset - Address early sticking
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, clean, and recalibrate VCT actuators; may resolve intermittent faults
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix if wear is minimal; not suitable for failed units

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Actuator Replacement - Address confirmed failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty VCT actuators with OEM-spec units; includes calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for confirmed failures; restores variable timing function

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt V12 - Not typically required
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: VCT failure does not typically cause internal engine damage
repairOptions.longevity: Actuator repair is separate from engine rebuild; address independently

problems.recommendation: If your Gen 2 Vanquish shows VCT fault codes or poor idle, have the actuators tested before replacing. Cleaning may resolve early sticking; confirmed failures require replacement (£1,500-£2,800).
problems.cta: Compare Aston Martin Vanquish VCT actuator repair quotes

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Aston Martin Vanquish
finalCta.paragraph: The Vanquish V12 is a complex, high-performance engine that demands specialist knowledge. Whether you're facing coil pack failures, timing chain wear, or oil surge concerns, EngineMarket connects you with accredited Aston Martin specialists who understand these engines. Compare rebuilt, reconditioned, and used V12 prices from trusted UK suppliers - every unit includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Aston Martin Vanquish Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Aston Martin Vanquish and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Aston Martin Vanquish Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Aston Martin Vanquish is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement unit.

groups.title: Aston Martin Vanquish Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Aston Martin Vanquish 5.9L Petrol Engines
items.description: The Aston Martin Vanquish exclusively uses a 5,935cc (5.9-litre) naturally aspirated V12 across all generations. This 60-degree V12, derived from Ford Duratec architecture but extensively re-engineered by Aston Martin, powered every Vanquish from the 2001 launch to the final 2018 Ultimarte. Output ranged from 460bhp (AM3) to 603bhp (AM29 Zagato), with evolution focused on internal strengthening, revised cylinder heads and ECU calibration rather than displacement changes.
commonFailurePoints: Wet sump oil surge on track
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin Vanquish 5.9L petrol engine prices

items.title: Aston Martin Vanquish Petrol Engines
items.description: Every Aston Martin Vanquish ever produced uses a 5.9-litre naturally aspirated V12 petrol engine, with output evolving from 460bhp (2001) to 603bhp (2018) across five engine codes.
items.descriptor: Every Aston Martin Vanquish ever produced uses a 5.9-litre naturally aspirated V12 petrol engine, with output evolving from 460bhp (2001) to 603bhp (2018) across five engine codes.
items.familiesLabel: Common Aston Martin Vanquish Petrol Engine Families
families: AM3 - First-gen base V12 (460bhp, 2001-2004), wet sump, prone to manifold cracks
AM4 - First-gen S V12 (520bhp, 2004-2007), forged internals, same vulnerabilities as AM3
AM11 - Second-gen base V12 (565bhp, 2012-2014), revised heads, VCT system introduced
AM28 - Second-gen late V12 (573bhp, 2014-2016), ECU calibration update only
AM29 - Second-gen S/Zagato V12 (595-603bhp, 2016-2018), highest output, limited parts availability
knownFor: Exclusively petrol V12 power across all variants and generations
Wet sump oil surge risk under high-G track use
Coil pack and timing chain wear as primary maintenance concerns
Matching-numbers provenance critical for Zagato, Ultimarte and S models
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin Vanquish Petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Aston Martin Vanquish Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Aston Martin Vanquish production timeline (2001-2018), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine code and compare rebuilt V12 prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original Vanquish debut introduced the AM3 6.0L V12, establishing Aston Martin's modern flagship grand tourer.
keyChanges: Launch of first-generation Vanquish with AM3 V12 (460bhp)
Wet sump lubrication system (track-use oil surge risk identified early)
knownFor: Groundbreaking design and performance for 2001
Exhaust manifold cracks common on high-mileage examples
Coil pack degradation typical after 60k miles
Matching-numbers provenance increasingly valuable for collectors
years.cta: Get quotes for 2001-2004 Aston Martin Vanquish replacement engines
years.ticker: Vanquish AM3 engine replacement • Vanquish exhaust manifold repair • Vanquish coil pack replacement cost

years.description: The Vanquish S upgraded the V12 to AM4 specification with forged internals, raising output to 520bhp.
keyChanges: Introduction of Vanquish S with AM4 V12 (520bhp)
Forged pistons, stronger conrods, revised camshafts
Vanquish Volante convertible launched alongside S specification
knownFor: 520bhp output represented significant performance gain over base Vanquish
Same wet sump and manifold vulnerabilities as AM3
Higher collector value for matching-numbers S and Volante examples
Coil pack and timing chain maintenance critical for reliability
years.cta: Compare Aston Martin Vanquish S 2004-2007 engine prices
years.ticker: Vanquish S AM4 engine replacement • Vanquish Volante V12 rebuild • Vanquish timing chain service cost

years.description: All-new Gaydon-built Vanquish introduced AM11 V12 with revised architecture, Touchtronic 2 transmission and improved lubrication.
keyChanges: New generation Vanquish on VH platform (Gaydon production)
AM11 V12 with 565bhp, revised cylinder heads, higher compression
Touchtronic 2 (ZF 6HP26) automatic transmission replacing electro-hydraulic manual
Revised lubrication system addressing Gen 1 oil surge concerns
mainEngines: AM11 - 5.9L petrol (Vanquish Gen 2, Vanquish Volante Gen 2, 2012-2014)
knownFor: Significant refinement and performance improvement over Gen 1
Variable cam timing (VCT) system introduced - new failure mode
Early VCT actuator failures reported on 2012-2013 models
Lower replacement demand than Gen 1 due to newer age and improved reliability
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2014 Aston Martin Vanquish replacement engines
years.ticker: Vanquish AM11 engine replacement • Vanquish Gen 2 VCT actuator repair • Vanquish Touchtronic 2 compatibility

years.description: AM28 engine code introduced with ECU calibration update only, raising output to 573bhp without mechanical changes.
keyChanges: AM28 engine code replaces AM11 (573bhp vs 565bhp)
Power increase achieved via ECU calibration only - identical hardware
Carbon Edition and other limited trims launched during this period
mainEngines: AM28 - 5.9L petrol (Vanquish Gen 2 Late, Vanquish Carbon Edition, 2014-2016)
knownFor: 8bhp gain over AM11 not perceptible in real-world driving
Same VCT and secondary air injection vulnerabilities as AM11
AM28 physically interchangeable with AM11 chassis - popular upgrade path
Lower replacement demand due to newer age and strong reliability record
years.cta: Compare Aston Martin Vanquish AM28 engine prices

years.description: Final Vanquish evolution introduced AM29 V12 with 595-603bhp, representing the highest-output factory Vanquish engine.
keyChanges: Vanquish S (2016-2018) debuts with AM29 V12 (595bhp standard, 603bhp Ultimarte)
Revised intake, exhaust, camshafts and ECU calibration
Vanquish Zagato limited edition (99 coupes, 99 Volante, 99 Shooting Brake) uses AM29 with unique calibration
Ultimarte (12 cars) represents final, most powerful factory Vanquish specification
mainEngines: AM29 - 5.9L petrol (Vanquish S Gen 2, Vanquish Zagato, Vanquish Ultimarte, 2016-2018)
knownFor: 603bhp output represents pinnacle of naturally aspirated Vanquish development
Sodium-filled exhaust valves and aggressive cam profiles require specialist maintenance
Extremely limited production (Zagato: 297 total, Ultimarte: 12) drives high collector value
Matching-numbers provenance critical; non-original engines significantly impact value
years.cta: Get quotes for 2016-2018 Aston Martin Vanquish S/Zagato replacement engines
years.ticker: Vanquish Zagato AM29 engine replacement • Vanquish Ultimarte V12 rebuild cost • Vanquish S Gen 2 engine specialist UK
enquiries: Vanquish S Gen 2 engine specialist UK

faq.h2: Aston Martin Vanquish Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does an Aston Martin Vanquish engine rebuild cost?
items.answer: A full Vanquish V12 rebuild typically costs £15,000-£45,000 depending on generation, engine code and whether you choose standard or upgraded components. First-gen AM3/AM4 rebuilds start around £15k, while final AM29 Zagato units can exceed £40k.
keyPoints: First-gen (AM3/AM4) rebuilds: £15,000-£25,000 typical range
Second-gen (AM11/AM28) rebuilds: £18,000-£32,000 typical range
AM29 Zagato/Ultimarte rebuilds: £25,000-£45,000 due to rarity and complexity
Prices include machining, parts, labour; exclude VAT and collection/delivery
Exchange engines may cost £3k-6k less but affect matching-numbers provenance
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Aston Martin Vanquish engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Can I upgrade my Vanquish AM11 engine to AM28 specification?
items.answer: Yes - AM11 and AM28 engines are mechanically identical; the 8bhp gain (565 to 573bhp) comes from ECU calibration only. A specialist remap costing £800-£1,500 can achieve the AM28 power level on an AM11 engine.
keyPoints: AM11 and AM28 share identical block, heads, internals and mounting points
Power difference is purely ECU calibration - no hardware changes required
Specialist remap costs £800-£1,500; 8bhp gain not perceptible in road use
Ensure tuner has Aston Martin V12 experience to avoid knock or damage
AM28 calibration may require updated emissions documentation for MOT
items.cta: Get quotes for Aston Martin Vanquish AM11 to AM28 ECU remap

items.question: What causes wet sump oil surge in Vanquish engines and how do I fix it?
items.answer: The Vanquish V12's wet sump system can allow oil to surge away from the pickup under high-G cornering or braking, causing momentary starvation and potential bearing damage. Fit an accusump (£800-£1,500) for occasional track use or a full dry sump conversion (£8k-£12k) for regular track driving.
keyPoints: Wet sump design is adequate for road use but vulnerable under sustained high-G loads
Accusump is an external oil accumulator that maintains pressure during surge events
Dry sump conversion replaces entire lubrication system - eliminates surge risk completely
Ignoring oil surge can cause bottom-end bearing failure requiring full rebuild
Track-day drivers should fit accusump minimum; serious competitors require dry sump
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin Vanquish oil surge prevention solutions

items.question: How do I preserve matching-numbers provenance when replacing my Vanquish engine?
items.answer: To maintain matching-numbers status, rebuild your original engine rather than swapping in a replacement unit. If a swap is unavoidable, retain your original block and have the engine number restamped with appropriate DVLA documentation.
keyPoints: Matching-numbers adds 10-20% to Vanquish value, especially for S, Zagato and Ultimarte
Rebuilding original block preserves provenance; exchange engines break the chain
If block is damaged beyond repair, restamping requires DVLA notification and specialist declaration
Keep all rebuild documentation, invoices and heritage certificates for future resale
Aston Martin Heritage Trust can verify original build records for provenance confirmation
items.cta: Get quotes for Aston Martin Vanquish matching-numbers engine rebuild

items.question: Are coil pack failures common on Vanquish V12 engines?
items.answer: Yes - all 12 coil packs on the Vanquish V12 are prone to heat-related degradation after 50k-80k miles. Replace the full set proactively (£800-£1,500) rather than waiting for misfires that can damage cylinders.
keyPoints: Vanquish V12 uses 12 individual coil packs (one per cylinder)
Heat soak in the tight V12 bay accelerates insulation breakdown
Misfires from failed coils can cause unburnt fuel to wash cylinder bores
Proactive full-set replacement at 60k miles prevents cascade failures
Use OEM-specification or upgraded coils; cheap aftermarket units fail prematurely
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin Vanquish coil pack replacement costs

items.question: What is the typical lifespan of a rebuilt Aston Martin Vanquish engine?
items.answer: A properly rebuilt Vanquish V12 with upgraded components addressing known failure points should deliver 40,000-60,000 miles of reliable service with regular maintenance. Lifespan depends on driving style, maintenance adherence and whether track use is involved.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines with upgraded bearings, pistons and oil system components last longer
Regular oil changes with correct 5W-40 fully synthetic specification are critical
Track use accelerates wear; accusump or dry sump recommended for circuit driving
Coil packs, tensioners and VCT actuators should be monitored/replaced preventatively
Full service history and specialist maintenance records support longevity and resale value
items.cta: Get quotes for Aston Martin Vanquish rebuilt engines with warranty

items.question: Can any Vanquish V12 engine fit my car regardless of year?
items.answer: No - first-generation (AM3/AM4, 2001-2007) and second-generation (AM11/AM28/AM29, 2012-2018) Vanquish engines are not directly interchangeable due to different engine mounts, ECUs and transmission interfaces.
keyPoints: Gen 1 (AM3/AM4) engines fit only Gen 1 chassis (2001-2007)
Gen 2 (AM11/AM28/AM29) engines fit only Gen 2 chassis (2012-2018)
Within each generation, engine codes are generally interchangeable (e.g., AM11 to AM28)
AM28 to AM29 upgrade requires intake, exhaust and ECU changes - not cost-effective
Always verify engine code compatibility with a Vanquish specialist before purchase
items.cta: Check Aston Martin Vanquish engine compatibility for your variant

items.question: How long does a Vanquish V12 engine rebuild take?
items.answer: Current lead times for a full Vanquish V12 rebuild range from 4-8 months, driven by specialist parts availability (pistons, gaskets, VCT actuators) and machine shop capacity at accredited Aston Martin workshops.
keyPoints: Lead times vary by specialist workload and parts supply chain status
Exchange engines may be available sooner but affect matching-numbers provenance
Plan for 6 months average; allow buffer for unexpected parts delays
Some specialists offer priority service for urgent cases at premium cost
Book early if you have a fixed deadline (e.g., concours event, sale)
items.cta: Get lead time estimates for Aston Martin Vanquish engine rebuilds

items.question: What warranty should I expect on a rebuilt Vanquish engine?
items.answer: Reputable UK specialists typically offer 12-24 month or 12,000-mile warranties on rebuilt Vanquish V12 engines, covering parts and labour. Always confirm whether electrical components (coil packs, sensors) are included.
keyPoints: Standard warranty: 12 months unlimited mileage on parts and labour
Premium rebuilds may include 24-month coverage at higher cost
Verify whether warranty covers electrical components and ancillaries
Keep all service records; warranty may require specialist maintenance intervals
EngineMarket network suppliers all provide minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare warranty terms on Aston Martin Vanquish rebuilt engines

items.question: Which UK specialists are best for Aston Martin Vanquish engine work?
items.answer: Leading accredited Vanquish V12 specialists include Aston Workshop (Baldock), Nicholas Mee (Hatfield), Chiltern Aston Centre (Hemel Hempstead) and Aston Engineering (Derby) - all Heritage Partners with factory-trained technicians and proven Vanquish rebuild experience.
keyPoints: Choose specialists with Aston Martin Heritage Partner accreditation
Verify specific Vanquish V12 rebuild experience (not just general Aston Martin work)
Request references or case studies of recent Vanquish rebuilds
Compare warranty terms, lead times and pricing across multiple quotes
EngineMarket connects you with vetted specialists for transparent price comparison
items.cta: Find accredited Aston Martin Vanquish engine specialists near you
